title: Feature Request: Add `selinuxLabel` Option in Podman Play Kube YAML
body:
**Description**

I ran the OpenSearch container with commands:

```bash

$ cd /home/arthur/Desktop

$ mkdir data1

$ podman unshare chown 1000:1000 data1

$ podman run \
  --name opensearch \
  -p 9200:9200 \
  -p 9600:9600 \
  -e discovery.type=single-node \
  -e plugins.security.disabled=true \
  -e bootstrap.memory_lock=true \
  -e OPENSEARCH_JAVA_OPTS="-Xms512m -Xmx512m" \
  -e OPENSEARCH_INITIAL_ADMIN_PASSWORD=YourSecurePassword123! \
  -v /home/arthur/Desktop/data1:/usr/share/opensearch/data:z \
  opensearchproject/opensearch:2.13.0

```

Then, I wanted to convert this setup to a pod yaml file for `podman play kube`, but I noticed the note in the [doc](https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman-kube-play.1.html)
> Note: hostPath volume types created by kube play is given an SELinux shared label (z), bind mounts are not relabeled (use chcon -t container_file_t -R <directory>).

This means that if we mount an existing host directory, we still need to manually run `chcon` on it beforehand, which may not be very intuitive for users unfamiliar with SELinux.


**Suggest potential solution**

Introduce a new `selinuxLabel` field in `volumeMounts` to represent the SELinux mount labeling behavior.

- `shared` corresponds to `:z`

- `private` corresponds to `:Z`

Example YAML

```yaml

apiVersion: v1
...
  containers:
    - name: opensearch
      image: opensearchproject/opensearch:2.13.0
      ...
      volumeMounts:
        - mountPath: /usr/share/opensearch/data
          name: opensearch-data
          selinuxLabel: shared # :z   <------------------- NEW!


  volumes:
    - name: opensearch-data
      hostPath:
        path: /home/arthur/Desktop/opensearch-data
        type: Directory

```
**Question**

Would this proposal be a reasonable enhancement? if so, I'd be happy to contribute this feature.

comments.body:
I generated my YAML from `podman kube generate $pod`, and saw that it represents SELinux label via `bind-mount-options`:

For example:

```
metadata:
  annotations:
    bind-mount-options: /home/arthur/Desktop/opensearch-data:z
```

This works for me, and avoids me needing to run `chcon`.

It's a little awkward having to duplicate the dir path though.
We cannot add new fields to the yaml that don't exist in kubernetes, this is why that annotation exists to set them.
